Lab Guide › Working in the Lab

Time off & vacation

How to request leave, sick days, and notice guidelines. Rest is encouraged.

Getting Started

  • Start guide: Undergraduates
  • Start guide: Graduate students
  • Start guide: Postdocs

  • Time off & vacation

Professional Development

  • Attending a conference
  • Applying for funding
  • Request recommendation letters
  • File your dissertation

You are welcome and encouraged to take vacation when and where you need it. If you aren’t taking any time off, you may be encouraged to at least take a stay-cation to reset. BIRD Lab members are strongly encouraged to take a minimum of 15 vacation days per calendar year — most members take 3–4 weeks.

The main ask: log your time off so people know when you’re unavailable.

How to request time off

We track vacation and leave with the Vacation Tracker app in Slack.

  1. In Slack, type /vacation
  2. Select your leave type, start date, and end date (reason optional)
  3. Click Send request
  4. Christina approves it in Slack, and it’s stored automatically for reference

The first time you submit a request, click Enable when prompted so the app can automatically set and clear your “Out of Office” Slack status.

Sick days

If you’re sick, please stay home and focus on recovering.

  • If you feel up to light work, optional low-lift tasks include reading papers, writing, or planning.
  • If not, fully log off — that’s okay.

Please log sick time through /vacation (specify Sick) so others know you’re out.

Notice guidelines

These are guidelines, not bureaucracy — the goal is to reduce surprises and keep projects moving.

Length of leave Suggested notice Before you go
Under 1 week At least 1 day in advance —
1–2 weeks About 1 month in advance Quick handoff: who to contact, time-sensitive tasks needing coverage, and set an out-of-office email reply
